name: Irish Soda Bread
recipeIngredient: 2/3 cup raisins, brown or golden or mixed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
2. In a large bowl, sift together the flour, baking soda, salt, nutmeg, and sugar.
3. Using a pastry blender or fingertips, work the shortening into the flour until the mixture resembles small peas.
4. Stir in the raisins and caraway seeds, mixing evenly.
5. Gradually stir in the buttermilk, about 1/4 cup at a time, using only enough to allow the dough to come together.
6. Knead the dough for 1-2 minutes.
7. Shape the dough into a round loaf and place on a greased baking sheet.
8. Cut an X across the top and around the sides of the loaf.
9. Bake for 45 minutes or until the bread sounds hollow when tapped.
10. Cool on a rack.
